strategies.intent_en: Industrial-compatible: minimal/no FR edits; focus on CMC and immunogenicity.
allowed_edits: CDR liabilities fix (deamidation/isoAsp oxidation hotspots) with minimal paratope perturbation
locked_regions: FR2 Vernier core (positions supporting CDR geometry)
Any residue flagged as paratope-contact (if known)
backmutation_rule: Only back-mutate FR residues if binding loss is observed or if canonical/cdr geometry mismatch is detected.

strategies.intent_en: Conditionally compatible: partitioned refinement; lock FR2/Vernier first.

actions: Identify Vernier candidates (FR2 & FR3 support positions) and freeze by default
If immunogenicity risk exists, propose conservative substitutions only on surface-exposed non-support positions
Remove/mitigate high-risk PTM motifs away from CDR contact zones
Back-mutate only a small set of FR2/Vernier positions if predicted geometry drift or affinity loss risk

strategies.intent_en: Function-coupled/OOD: avoid framework swap; do function-preserving de-immunization and point patches.
allowed_edits: Single-point edits on solvent-exposed, non-support positions with clear risk reduction
Stability patches (e.g., reduce hydrophobic surface) only if not in Vernier/support
De-immunization edits prioritized by risk-weighting and minimal structural impact
validation_requirements: Stage-gated: binding/biophysical check after every small edit set
If available, require structural model confirmation before touching Vernier/support positions

SOLUBLE_PROTEIN.en: Typically less geometry-constrained; prefer low-risk scaffold strategies.

MEMBRANE_ECD.en: Angle/conformation constraints are common; Vernier/FR2 are sensitive—use partitioned refinement.

ENZYME_ACTIVE_SITE.en: Deep pockets often require framework contributions; avoid wholesale swaps.
